title: Cicada Symphony: A Rare Double Emergence
story: Buzz! Buzz! In 2024, the air fills with an incredible sound as trillions of cicadas burst from the ground. This May 31 marks a special event: two different groups of these noisy insects appear at the same time. It's the first double emergence in 221 years! The Great Southern Brood, asleep for 13 years, joins another group that's been waiting 17 years. Together, they create a once-in-a-lifetime cicada concert across parts of the United States.
activity_question: How long had the Great Southern Brood of cicadas been asleep?
bonus_fact: Cicadas are harmless to humans and can't bite or sting. They're just noisy!

title: Lightning Bolt Strikes the Track
story: Zoom! In 2008, Usain Bolt lived up to his name, blazing across the finish line in an incredible 9.72 seconds. This lightning-fast sprint on May 31 shattered the world record for the 100-meter dash. Bolt's speed was no fluke - the wind speed was within legal limits, making his feat even more impressive. The Jamaican sprinter's jaw-dropping performance left spectators and fellow athletes in awe.
activity_question: What was Usain Bolt's record-breaking time in the 100-meter sprint?

title: Ancient City Emerges from Drought
story: Archaeologists made an incredible discovery in 2022 when a lost Bronze Age city emerged from the Tigris River in Iraq. A severe drought revealed the 3,400-year-old ruins, likely part of the ancient Mittani Empire. Researchers rushed to explore the site, unearthing about a hundred clay tablets covered in cuneiform writing. These ancient texts, preserved for millennia, offer a glimpse into life on May 31 thousands of years ago.
activity_question: What type of writing was found on the clay tablets?
follow_up_question: Why do you think droughts can sometimes help archaeologists make new discoveries?

title: Superfan's Sky-High Baseball Stunt
story: Charley Lupica took his love for baseball to new heights in 1949. On May 31, this Cleveland Indians superfan climbed a 60-foot pole and settled onto a tiny platform at the top. Charley vowed to stay up there until his team won the league championship. His sky-high stunt lasted an incredible 117 days, but sadly, the Indians didn't clinch the victory he hoped for.
activity_question: What did Charley Lupica vow to do?
activity_choices: Stay on the platform until the Indians won
Climb the pole every day for a year
Paint the pole Cleveland Indians colors
Sing the team's anthem from the platform
activity_answer: Stay on the platform until the Indians won
follow_up_question: How do you think Charley felt when he finally came down after 117 days?

title: The Great Johnstown Flood
story: Water roared through Johnstown, Pennsylvania in 1889, changing lives forever. The South Fork Dam on the Little Conemaugh River suddenly broke on May 31, unleashing a massive flood. The town below had little warning. While many lives were sadly lost, heroes emerged. Neighbors helped each other to safety, showing incredible bravery. This event led to improved dam safety rules across America.
activity_question: What river was the South Fork Dam located on?
follow_up_question: How might early warning systems help prevent similar disasters today?

title: Tommy Lasorda's Pitching Masterpiece
story: Batter up! On May 31, 1948, Tommy Lasorda stepped onto the mound for the Schenectady Blue Jays. The young pitcher was about to make baseball history. title: Brave Youth Stand Up for Equality
story: Hungry for change, young activists in Oklahoma City took bold action in 1963. On May 31, members of the NAACP Youth Council began sitting at segregated lunch counters, refusing to leave until served. Their peaceful protests, called sit-ins, challenged unfair rules keeping people apart based on skin color. The brave youths efforts paid off! Almost two dozen businesses soon changed their policies, agreeing to serve everyone equally.

activity_choices: They began sit-ins at lunch counters
activity_answer: They began sit-ins at lunch counters
follow_up_question: Why do you think peaceful protests like sit-ins can be effective in creating change?

title: NASA's UFO Hunt Takes Off
story: Aliens or microwaves? NASA's first open meeting on unidentified flying objects in 2023 revealed some surprising challenges. title: A Bridge to Adventure
story: Imagine zooming across the sea without a boat! In 1997, the Confederation Bridge opened, connecting Prince Edward Island to New Brunswick. It's like a giant roller coaster for vehicles!
activity_question: What did the Confederation Bridge replace?
bonus_fact: The Confederation Bridge is the longest bridge over ice-covered water in the world.

activity_question: What was Ramesses II also known as?

title: Golf Balls Soar from Alaskan Peak
story: Saul Ballesteros took golf to new heights in 1987. On May 31, he climbed to the top of Mt. McKinley, Alaska's tallest mountain. title: Secret Space Race Begins
story: Hidden in the vast Kazakh steppe, a secret project was taking shape. On May 31, 1955, Soviet engineers broke ground on a massive new launch facility. This remote site would soon become the heart of the Soviet space program. Known as the Baikonur Cosmodrome, it would launch the first satellite and the first human into space, igniting the space race.
activity_question: What was being built in the Kazakh steppe?
bonus_fact: Baikonur Cosmodrome is still used today for space missions.

title: A Cosmic Visitor Zooms Past Earth
story: Zoom! A celestial traveler raced through space, coming closer to Earth than most comets ever dare. On May 31, 1930, Comet 73P, also known as Schwassmann-Wachmann 3, swooped by our planet at an incredibly close distance. This cosmic snowball approached within 0.0617 astronomical units of Earth, giving astronomers an exciting chance to study it up close.
activity_question: What was the name of the comet that came close to Earth?
follow_up_question: What does AU stand for in astronomy?

title: A Dutch Hero's Brave Beginning
story: In a small Dutch town, a future hero took his first breath. Johan Brouwer was born in Delfshaven, Netherlands on May 31, 1898. He grew up to become a talented writer and an expert on Spanish culture. But Brouwer's most important role was yet to come. During World War II, he bravely fought against the Nazis as part of the Dutch resistance.
activity_question: What did Johan Brouwer become an expert on?
follow_up_question: Why do you think it's important to stand up against unfair treatment, like Brouwer did?

title: Explorer's Birth Shapes Patagonia's Future
story: A curious baby boy named Francisco Moreno opened his eyes in Buenos Aires, Argentina in 1852. Little did anyone know this newborn would grow up to become a famous explorer! Moreno's adventures in Patagonia, a wild region in southern South America, helped people learn about this mysterious land. On May 31, the same day he was born, Moreno's legacy lives on through the La Plata Museum of Natural History, which he founded.
activity_question: What did Francisco Moreno found?
activity_choices: The La Plata Museum of Natural History
activity_answer: The La Plata Museum of Natural History
follow_up_question: What region did Moreno explore that helped people learn about a mysterious land?

title: A Musical Pioneer's First Notes
story: Louise Farrenc's musical journey began in Paris on May 31, 1804. She grew up to become a talented pianist and composer, breaking barriers along the way. In a time when women rarely held such positions, Farrenc became the only female professor at the prestigious Paris Conservatory during the 1800s. Her melodies and teachings continue to inspire musicians today.
activity_question: What unique position did Louise Farrenc hold at the Paris Conservatory?
follow_up_question: Can you name another instrument besides the piano that composers often play?

title: A Lucky King's Birth
story: A royal baby's cry echoed through Alcochete, Portugal on May 31, 1469. This newborn would grow up to become King Manuel I, nicknamed 'The Fortunate'. Manuel ruled during an exciting time of exploration when Portuguese ships sailed to far-off lands. His reign saw Portugal become a powerful empire, stretching across oceans.
activity_question: What was King Manuel I's nickname?
follow_up_question: Why do you think King Manuel I was called 'The Fortunate'?
